whatWasImplemented: supabase/migrations/20260419080000_leads_escalation_fields.sql — leads gains phone text, extra_fields jsonb (object CHECK), source text CHECK ('widget'|'escalation') default 'widget'. email dropped NOT NULL so phone-only leads are legal. leads_contact_present CHECK enforces email OR phone present. UNIQUE(site_id, email) replaced with a partial unique index leads_site_email_unique_partial WHERE email IS NOT NULL so phone-only duplicates don't collide.

src/app/api/leads/route.ts — accepts phone, source, extra_fields. email_or_phone_required replaces the old 'email is required' 400. Email-provided rows upsert on (site_id, email); phone-only rows insert as new distinct leads. source validated against enum; extra_fields coerced to {} when missing.

widget/src/escalation-protocol.ts — pure helpers: parseStream() splits a buffered body on \x1E and JSON-parses the trailer (null on malformed). buildShowFormPayload() builds the /api/leads body from raw form values, promoting email/phone/name to top-level while preserving everything in extra_fields so the dashboard can surface custom-field data per lead.

widget/src/widget.tsx — rewrote chat panel. Removes the hardcoded 'after 3 messages show email capture' (replaced by turn_count escalation rules). Stream reader strips the trailer live: as soon as \x1E appears in the buffer, only the preceding bytes are shown as assistant text. On stream close, parseStream() yields the pending_action. EscalationView dispatches to five renderers: AskEmailForm, AskPhoneForm, ShowForm (dynamic inputs from action_config.fields), CalendlyEmbed (100% x 600px iframe guarded against non-http URLs), HandoffIndicator (role=status with animated dots and 'A team member will be with you shortly.').

Escalation views share CSS in the shadow-DOM getStyles() block — .rc-escalation base, .rc-escalation-calendly (no padding for iframe), .rc-escalation-handoff (indigo pill with animated dots that honor prefers-reduced-motion).
src/app/dashboard/conversations/page.tsx — row query includes needs_human, card shows a ⚑ handoff amber pill when true (VAL-ESCAL-015 dashboard side).

src/__tests__/widget-escalation-protocol.test.ts — 8 tests for parseStream (no sentinel, JSON hit, malformed JSON, empty trailer, action_config round-trip) + buildShowFormPayload (promotion, empty-skipping, empty input).
src/__tests__/leads-route.test.ts — extended to cover email_or_phone_required, invalid source rejection, source=escalation upsert flag, phone-only insert path, show_form extra_fields preservation.
whatWasLeftUndone: Playwright evidence for each of the five action renderers (VAL-ESCAL-011..015 visual assertions) and the end-to-end stream-to-widget flow. The logic is unit-tested at the boundaries (pure parseStream + buildShowFormPayload + leads route) and defers to Playwright for visual + DOM snapshots per the M2-M6 pattern.
Did not install @testing-library/preact as a dev dep — extracted pure helpers instead so the main Vitest harness covers them. Revisit if the widget grows significantly.
discoveredIssues: leads.email NOT NULL + UNIQUE(site_id,email) did not accommodate phone-only leads. Migration relaxes both invariants with a partial unique index and a leads_contact_present CHECK. Backfill not needed — all existing rows have emails.

The widget needs to strip the \x1E trailer AS IT STREAMS so the user doesn't momentarily see raw JSON before the stream closes. Solved by checking indexOf on each chunk and trimming the 'visible' slice.

verification.testsRun: pnpm vitest run — 555 passed / 1 skipped / 67 files (+13 new: 8 protocol + 5 extended leads).

manualChecks.expected: ⚑ handoff pill renders before visitor id

filesChanged: widget/src/widget.tsx (rewrote — pending_action parsing + 5 escalation renderers, removes hardcoded 3-turn email capture)
src/__tests__/leads-route.test.ts (extended — 5 new tests + existing email-missing reframed)
